title: stachys swainsonii subsp. swainsonii
intro: Stachys swainsonii subsp. swainsonii, also known as mountain betony, is a perennial plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The medicinal properties of mountain betony also provide health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, mountain betony is often used as a tea, tincture, or infrequent addition to salves and creams, and can also be used as a culinary herb in small quantities to add flavor to various dishes.
